headline.text: L1 Sollich 4 Theegarten has fought wax-paper wrapping all shift — *1 in 20 wrapped properly* at worst, still adjusting at window close.

per_plant_summary.L1: L1 ran caramel on S2 and S4. S2 had a broken MM8 bag-opening finger at 22:27 (part detectable by x-ray, running with 3 fingers). S3 cleared open-pouch issues by 13:22 but shorted the order. S4 struggled with Theegarden wax-paper changeover from 17:38 onward — caramel flung everywhere, only 1 in 20 wrapping properly at worst — maintenance still adjusting at window close. C20 belt and decorator allergen tests pending as of 23:50.
per_plant_summary.L2: L2 ran VFFS and two molding lines. VFFS had four brief stops (BB date, open gusset, empty pouches, film tear) all cleared same shift, longest 34 minutes. ML2 had two flow-wrap jams cleared in minutes. ML3 had glue buildup, filler sensor, flow-wrap, x-ray, and belt issues all cleared same problem. Both molding lines ran smoothly after early morning clears.
per_plant_summary.L3: L3 ML1 fought tempering instability all day — low slope at start, swinging high and low, stopping/starting deposition six times, last stop at 06:48 with restart at 07:14. Flow-wrap open-seal rate improved from 5.7% to 0.28% by 14:09. Belt C9 melted chocolate at 14:01 due to closed cooling valve (opened, fixed). Cavanna chain pin fell at 17:42, ran intermittently rest of shift with sealing and cutting issues still live at 23:14. ML4 had TG4/5 film-cutting problems from 09:10 through window close, dirty-mold shortage at 23:25 (caught up by hand-washing), and nozzle issue cleared at 05:13. Truffle line ran out early due to seal damage, curtain down at 22:43. MF Matrix had bracket fall off at 20:03, no fix posted.

notes.text: Grey Boscan tracked ML1 buffer-tank draw-down rate to plan second-shift stop time (Chad praised proactive shutdown planning at 08:11).
Katya flagged tool-cabinet health check at 07:45: tools missing or swapped, no return times logged.
Maria Moron Quintero clarified ML3 needs 2.68M more dark pieces before milk changeover (target 9.98M on-hand).
Bequet salted caramel run (S4) used custom item 30923-Box22 at 883 pieces/case instead of standard 891.

resolved.title: S2 MM8 bag-opening finger snapped off
resolved.summary: MM8 bag-opening finger fell off and snapped at 22:27; operator stopped, part appears complete.
resolved.resolution: Piece confirmed detectable by x-ray at 22:41; running with 3 fingers temporarily, hourly x-ray checks clean (Delia Zambrano, Elizabeth Hernandez).

resolved.title: S3 open pouches at top hole
resolved.summary: Multiple pouches open at small hole at top of packaging found at 13:17.
resolved.resolution: Line stopped and run out by 13:22 — operators notified, issue rectified, order 1,395 pouches short (Nathan Topham).

resolved.title: VFFS bags coming out empty
resolved.summary: Bags empty at 14:29, maintenance checking depositor/filler.
resolved.resolution: Up and running at 15:05 — downtime 2:20 to 2:33, 13 minutes (Brandon Lance).

resolved.title: VFFS open gusset recurring
resolved.summary: Line down at 07:37 for open gusset, Dave from Syntegon assisting maintenance.
resolved.resolution: Line running at 08:21 — gusset fixed, 44-minute stop (Jeffrey Barney).

resolved.title: ML3 glue buildup on case-erector rollers
resolved.summary: Line stopped at 07:50, too much glue on rollers.
resolved.resolution: Line running at 08:02 — rollers cleaned, 12-minute stop (Jeffrey Barney).

resolved.title: ML2 flow-wrap jam, twin-belt sensor
resolved.summary: Line down at 08:42 for flow-wrap jam, then again at 08:56 for twin-belt sensor adjustment.
resolved.resolution: Line running at 08:57 — sensor adjusted, jams cleared, combined 15-minute stop (ML2 FW).

resolved.title: ML3 flow-wrap label misalignment causing opens
resolved.summary: Line down at 19:41 for flow-wrap issues; label going to one side, sending opened chocolates.
resolved.resolution: Line running at 19:47 — flow wrap adjusted, 6-minute stop (Molding Line 3 Pack).

resolved.title: ML3 x-ray showing false 'bin full' error
resolved.summary: Line down at 21:50, x-ray sending error code saying bin full when empty.
resolved.resolution: Line running at 22:11 — x-ray reset, 21-minute stop (Brandon Lance explains issue).

resolved.title: VFFS film tear at shoulder, printer missing numbers
resolved.summary: Film tore at shoulder at 22:39; gusset hoses and container overfull, believed to be cause.
resolved.resolution: Line up at 23:51 — film replaced, debris removed from print head (Gustavo Rodas).

resolved.title: VFFS best-by date issue at startup
resolved.summary: Line down at 07:17, quality flagged BB date problem.
resolved.resolution: Line running at 07:21 — date corrected, 4-minute stop (Jeffrey Barney).

resolved.title: ML3 belt before detector
resolved.summary: Line stopped at 17:48, adjusting belt before detector.
resolved.resolution: Line running at 17:55 — belt fixed, 7-minute stop (ML2 FW).

resolved.title: ML3 filler sensor issue
resolved.summary: Line down at 18:31, small filler sensor problem.
resolved.resolution: Line started running at 18:34 — sensor reset, 3-minute stop (Brandon Lance confirms).

resolved.title: Belt C9 melting chocolate due to closed cooling valve
resolved.summary: Chocolate melting on C9 belt at 14:01; Nelson Cook found cooling-water valve closed, no circulation through chill plates.
resolved.resolution: Valve opened at 14:14, plates cooled down; action added to startup checklist to verify valve open (Grey Boscan).

resolved.title: ML4 dirty-mold shortage, 10 totes waiting
resolved.summary: Line stopping frequently at 23:25 due to dirty molds; 10 totes waiting, no clean ones available.
resolved.resolution: Caught up by 00:19 with 2 EEs hand-washing plus ATP pass-off as cleaned; expectation set at 1 tote/hour (Marcos Montano).

resolved.title: Flow-wrap open-seal rate high at 5.7%
resolved.summary: Open-unit rate on ML1 flow wrap hit 5.7% at 09:40; speed reduced to 15mpm and cut position adjusted.
resolved.resolution: Cleared by 14:09 — rate dropped to 0.28% (3 open out of 1,056) after adjustments, "well below 1%" (David Zuniaga).

resolved.title: Metal detector belt slipped on ML1
resolved.summary: MD belt slipped at 15:17, line down.

resolved.title: Truffle line seal damage causing chocolate markings
resolved.summary: Chocolates showing markings at 16:09; inspection found seal hanging off line.
resolved.resolution: Line run out by 17:28, cleaning and tarp takedown completed, curtain down at 22:43 (Nathan Gevenois).

resolved.title: ML4 nozzle depositing halfway on every mold
resolved.summary: One WDS nozzle depositing halfway on same cavity every mold at 02:51.
resolved.resolution: Nozzle removed and cleaned at 05:13 — "no further issues with it" (Kimberly Morales).
